findings.title: Test code in production: 2 issues
findings.description:
Test utilities and patterns found in what appears to be production code.

**Found:**
• Mock/stub objects (lines 76, 77)
⚠️ This file is imported by production code!
findings.suggested_fix:
Options:

1. **Move to test files:** If this is test code, move it to a test directory
2. **Use TYPE_CHECKING:** For type hints only, wrap imports in `if TYPE_CHECKING:`
3. **Environment check:** If needed at runtime, guard with environment variable

```python
# Instead of:
from unittest.mock import Mock

# Use:
from typing import TYPE_CHECKING
if TYPE_CHECKING:
from unittest.mock import Mock
```
findings.why_it_matters: This test code is imported by production code. Test dependencies add bloat, security risks, and can cause unexpected behavior in production.

findings.title: Architectural Bottleneck: addRoute (architectural hub)
findings.description:
Function 'addRoute' is called by 25 functions and has complexity 22. Changes here are high-risk.

Betweenness centrality: 0.5730 - this function sits on many execution paths between other functions.

📊 Threshold: 20 (adaptive) | Actual: 22 | Default would be: 20 | Profile: p90=5, p95=9, mean=2.7, n=525
findings.suggested_fix: This is a critical hub. Ensure comprehensive test coverage, add defensive error handling, and consider circuit breaker pattern.
findings.why_it_matters: Bottlenecks are single points of failure that amplify bugs. High fan-in with high complexity means many callers depend on risky code.

findings.title: Mutual recursion: 4 functions in call cycle (complexity 8)
findings.description: Mutual recursion detected: 4 functions form a call cycle. Cycle: /tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/gin/context.go::(*Context).GetQuery:554 -> /tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/gin/context.go::(*Context).GetQueryArray:580 -> /tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/gin/context.go::(*Context).Query:525 -> /tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/gin/context.go::(*Context).initQueryCache:568. Aggregate complexity: 8.
findings.suggested_fix: Break the cycle by extracting shared logic into a common helper, introducing an event system, or restructuring the call chain to be unidirectional.
findings.why_it_matters: Mutual recursion creates tight coupling between functions, making them impossible to understand, test, or refactor independently. It can also cause stack overflows if the recursion depth is unbounded.

findings.title: Long method: getValue (247 lines)
findings.description: [COMPOUND: 2 co-located issues] Function 'getValue' has 247 lines (effective threshold: 150).
findings.why_it_matters: Long methods are hard to understand, test, and maintain. Each function should do one thing well.

findings.title: Excessive nesting: 9 levels in getValue
findings.description:
[COMPOUND: 2 co-located issues] 9 levels of nesting (threshold: 7).

**Analysis:**
In function: `getValue`
High complexity: 63 (nesting compounds this)

Threshold: 5 (adaptive) | Actual: 9 | Default would be: 5 | Profile: p90=2, p95=3, mean=0.7, n=525
findings.suggested_fix: Extract nested logic into functions or use early returns.
findings.why_it_matters: Deep nesting makes code hard to read and maintain. Each level increases cognitive load exponentially.

findings.title: Long method: findCaseInsensitivePathRec (247 lines)
findings.why_it_matters: Long methods are hard to understand, test, and maintain. Each function should do one thing well.

findings.title: Excessive nesting: 8 levels in findCaseInsensitivePathRec
findings.description:
8 levels of nesting (threshold: 7).

**Analysis:**
In function: `findCaseInsensitivePathRec`
High complexity: 69 (nesting compounds this)
Existing helpers that could reduce nesting: shiftNRuneBytes

Threshold: 5 (adaptive) | Actual: 8 | Default would be: 5 | Profile: p90=2, p95=3, mean=0.7, n=525
findings.suggested_fix:
This function already has helpers like `shiftNRuneBytes`. Consider:
1. Extract more nested blocks into similar helpers
2. Use guard clauses (early returns) to reduce nesting
3. Replace nested ifs with switch/match
findings.why_it_matters: Deep nesting makes code hard to read and maintain. Each level increases cognitive load exponentially.
